raw: {"weights":{"R1.1":0.3,"R1.2":0.7,"R1.3":0.7,"R1.4":0.3,"R1.5":0.0,"R1.6":0.5,"R1.7":0.5,"RX.1":1.0,"RX.2":1.0,"RX.3":1.0,"RX.4":1.0,"RX.5":1.0},"active_set":["R1.2","R1.3","R1.6","R1.7","RX.1","RX.2","RX.3","RX.4","RX.5"],"rationale":{"R1.2":"Any 'check' of a research artifact naturally includes verifying that the logical chain from premise (WER limitations) through intermediate claims (2.4× underestimation factor, 4/6 rank disagreements) to the contribution claim (r=0.91 composite metric) is coherent and non-circular.","R1.3":"The excerpt makes specific quantitative assertions (r=0.91 vs 0.62, 2.4× factor, 4 of 6 systems) tied to a described experimental pipeline; a 'check' plausibly requires verifying those numbers are internally consistent and traceable to the stated methodology.","R1.6":"One common reading of 'check this' is a prose/editorial pass; the artifact is a polished research excerpt, making writing quality a plausible primary responsibility target.","R1.7":"The excerpt attributes foundational ideas to Wang et al., Kim and Park, Ramirez et al., and Chen et al. — a 'check' plausibly extends to verifying these attributions are accurate, non-duplicative, and sufficiently comprehensive."},"clarification_needed":true,"clarification_question":"Should 'check this' be interpreted primarily as a scientific/technical review (verifying logical consistency and evidence-claim alignment) or primarily as an editorial review (assessing prose quality and clarity)?"}

rationale.R1.2: Any 'check' of a research artifact naturally includes verifying that the logical chain from premise (WER limitations) through intermediate claims (2.4× underestimation factor, 4/6 rank disagreements) to the contribution claim (r=0.91 composite metric) is coherent and non-circular.
rationale.R1.3: The excerpt makes specific quantitative assertions (r=0.91 vs 0.62, 2.4× factor, 4 of 6 systems) tied to a described experimental pipeline; a 'check' plausibly requires verifying those numbers are internally consistent and traceable to the stated methodology.
rationale.R1.6: One common reading of 'check this' is a prose/editorial pass; the artifact is a polished research excerpt, making writing quality a plausible primary responsibility target.
rationale.R1.7: The excerpt attributes foundational ideas to Wang et al., Kim and Park, Ramirez et al., and Chen et al. — a 'check' plausibly extends to verifying these attributions are accurate, non-duplicative, and sufficiently comprehensive.

parsed.clarification_question: Should 'check this' be interpreted primarily as a scientific/technical review (verifying logical consistency and evidence-claim alignment) or primarily as an editorial review (assessing prose quality and clarity)?
